origin: {name} was buried outside {homeland}, then clawed back before dawn.
The plague pits of {homeland} spat out {name}, and nobody welcomed them home.
A failed funeral in {homeland} ended with {name} breathing again.
fracture: {scar} is where the grave nails held too long.
Only {trinket} came back from the pit with them.
{notesHook}
drive: {name} follows every rumor of a saint who can explain why they returned.
They keep moving, certain the grave wants payment for lost time.
Their vow is simple: leave every crypt emptier than they found it.
doom: Dogs howl before {name} enters a village.
Each new moon, they wake with soil under their nails.
Someday the earth will remember where to put them.

origin: {name} served the altar until the altar fed someone they loved to fire.
Once praised as {epithet}, {name} was cast out after asking the wrong prayer.
{name} walked from {homeland} with scripture ash in their lungs.
fracture: They keep {trinket} as proof that faith can rot.
{scar} was burned in as a warning to other doubters.
Their old order posted a bounty and called it absolution.
drive: Now they hunt apostates, bishops, and liars with equal zeal.
They chase every rumor of the priest who signed the execution list.
Their only prayer now is made with {weapon} drawn.
doom: When bells ring at dusk, {name} counts exits before doors.
A choir follows in dreams, always one note short of mercy.
Sooner or later, the old church will send someone they cannot kill.

origin: {name} watched {homeland} collapse under carts of the dead.
The fever took everyone in {homeland} except {name}.
In the quarantine fires, {name} learned to sleep with smoke in their teeth.
fracture: {scar} was carved while fever dreams argued over their fate.
Their quarantine tag was replaced by {trinket}, hidden under cloth.
No innkeeper forgets their face for long.
drive: They gather coin for medicine they no longer trust.
They escort the sick when nobody else will touch the job.
They never pass a burning pyre without checking the names.
doom: Each cough nearby sounds like a prophecy.
They can smell sickness before the first symptom appears.
One day they will realize the plague never left them.

origin: {name} marched as {className} until the command turned butchery into duty.
A border war made {name} famous in {homeland} for all the wrong reasons.
Their company vanished in swamp fog, leaving {name} and silence.
fracture: {scar} came from a captain's blade when they refused one last order.
{trinket} is the only banner scrap they did not burn.
The quartermaster sold their name and called it paperwork.
drive: They take mercenary work only against tyrants and cults.
They track surviving squadmates to settle old debts in person.
They refuse to sleep twice in the same camp.
doom: The sound of marching feet still wakes them armed.
Someone always recognizes their face from wanted posters.
The war ended, but their sentence did not.

origin: {name} wandered into the marsh near {homeland} and returned hearing voices in water.
After three nights lost in black reeds, {name} came back with {epithet}.
{name} was found at dawn, kneeling in mud around {trinket}.
fracture: {scar} glows pale whenever storms gather.
Neighbors in {homeland} call them cursed, then pay for prophecies.
Even sober, they hear frogs chant funeral hymns.
drive: They seek the drowned oracle that whispered their true name.
They trade omens for shelter, coin, and information.
They follow floodwater trails to graves nobody mapped.
doom: Reflections show a different face if looked at too long.
Rain always starts when they lie.
One day the marsh will ask for its messenger back.

origin: {name} learned to rob catacombs before learning to read.
In {homeland}, tomb work paid better than honest labor.
{name} made a living selling saints in pieces to desperate nobles.
fracture: {trinket} was never meant to leave its shrine.
{scar} came from a trap that should have ended them.
A rival gang marked their name in blood on chapel doors.
drive: They want one final score large enough to disappear forever.
They now steal relics only to return them for a price.
They hunt maps to ruined vaults beneath {homeland}.
doom: Priests and thieves both want them dead.
Every relic they carry feels heavier at sunset.
The dead seem to know exactly what was taken.

origin: {name} survived a witch trial by naming names no one could verify.
They fled {homeland} when the magistrate ran out of neighbors to burn.
The gallows snapped, and {name} called it permission to keep living.
fracture: {scar} is where the noose bit deepest.
Their only witness now is {trinket}, blackened by court fire.
The magistrate's seal still appears on bounty notices.
drive: They expose inquisitors town by town, one confession at a time.
They protect hedge-witches and charlatans alike from rope justice.
They promised to drag every false judge into daylight.
doom: Crowds get quiet when they enter a square.
Any bonfire smell makes their hands shake.
The same judge keeps appearing in their dreams, always smiling.

origin: {name} rang death bells in {homeland} until nobody was left to mourn.
When plague came, {name} became {epithet} by counting corpses aloud.
The belfry collapsed, and only {name} walked out.
fracture: {scar} formed the night the clapper split and shrapnel filled the tower.
They keep {trinket} because it still hums at midnight.
Their hearing never recovered; silence sounds like threats.
drive: They follow rumors of cursed bells that summon the dead.
They record every battlefield name in a ledger nobody may read.
They refuse to let the forgotten die unnamed.
doom: Whenever church bells ring, {name} tastes iron.
Birds drop from rafters when they sing old hymns.
The next bell they ring might be their own.

origin: {name} drove corpse carts through {homeland} until the roads learned their face.
A magistrate in {homeland} paid {name} in silence and stale bread to haul the dead.
The last winter in {homeland}, {name} stacked bodies higher than wagon rails.
fracture: {scar} came from a wheel pin snapping downhill into a ditch of bones.
They kept {trinket} from a pauper no priest would bless.
Now every rattling axle sounds like a prayer unfinished.
drive: They seek roads no plague cart ever returned from.
They bury strangers for coin and enemies for free.
They swear no corpse under their watch will feed crows.
doom: Wheels creak in the dark even when their cart stands still.
Mules refuse to cross bridges after seeing {name}.
One night, their old cargo will sit up and ask for names.

origin: {name} studied rites beneath cracked altar stones in {homeland}.
Once called {epithet}, {name} was sent to cast out a devil and returned alone.
{name} burned their own prayer book after the bells would not stop.
fracture: {scar} marks where holy oil caught fire on skin.
Their order gave them {trinket} and called it pardon.
They still mouth old liturgies when thunder begins.
drive: They hunt whispers of possessions no bishop dares name.
They gather forbidden rites to finish the exorcism that failed.
They offer mercy first, steel second, and flame last.
doom: Children smile at empty corners when {name} enters a room.
Candles gutter black wherever they sleep.
The thing they failed to cast out still knows their true name.

origin: {name} learned the salt roads of {homeland} by outrunning tax blades.
Before the wars, {name} smuggled flour and relics between starving towns.
{name} crossed marsh ferries at midnight with sacks and lies.
fracture: {scar} came from customs men with hooked pikes.
They hid {trinket} inside a cargo of bones and never sold it.
A drowned partner still knocks on their dreams for unpaid shares.
drive: They move contraband medicine to villages written off as dead.
They seek one last route through the old checkpoints.
They trade secrets faster than goods and trust neither.
doom: Every toll bell sounds like a verdict.
Dogs along the road bark before {name} appears.
Soon, every border post will carry their likeness in charcoal.

origin: {name} lit oil lamps in catacombs beneath {homeland} where nobles hid their dead.
As a child, {name} memorized every tunnel where the lantern smoke would not rise.
{name} kept vigil underground while cities above forgot the names below.
fracture: {scar} formed when hot oil spilled during a cave-in.
They found {trinket} in a chamber sealed with wax and teeth.
Darkness no longer frightens them; silence does.
drive: They map forgotten ossuaries before looters can reach them.
They guide pilgrims through tombs for coin and confession.
They swear to return every stolen bone to its shelf.
doom: Their lamp burns low whenever the dead are restless.
Bats roost in rooms where {name} says no word.
One night, the tunnels will close behind them for good.

origin: {name} was crowned a miracle-worker in {homeland} after a lucky lie.
Pilgrims touched {name}'s hands and called blisters stigmata.
The title {epithet} began as mockery and became a procession.
fracture: {scar} came from zealots demanding proof in iron.
They wear {trinket} because followers would kill for it.
When famine struck, their miracles failed and the mob remembered.
drive: They seek a true sign to justify every false sermon.
They steal relics to keep worse hands from wielding them.
They feed shrines with silver and leave before dawn.
doom: Crowds still kneel when they should run.
Any blessing they speak now sounds like a curse.
One day their oldest devotee will come to collect salvation.

origin: {name} stitched soldiers in mud camps outside {homeland} until thread ran out.
Armies hired {name} for quick hands and fewer questions.
In siege winters, {name} traded surgery for firewood and prayers.
fracture: {scar} marks where a patient woke with a knife still inside.
They keep {trinket} from the first life they failed to save.
The smell of vinegar and blood never leaves their coat.
drive: They search for herbs that might undo old battlefield fevers.
They take any contract that keeps scalpels in hand.
They hunt the general who ordered wounded men burned.
doom: Hands shake only when the wound is familiar.
They hear groans at night from beds long empty.
One day they will face a patient they cannot cut away from memory.
